summary: Malicious npm package: obfuscated browser payload exfiltrates page HTML and hijacks WordPress accounts on noviembrenacional.com
details: The package's main file is an obfuscated (unicode-escape + reversed-string) browser payload with no legitimate functionality. When loaded in a page whose URL contains noviembrenacional.com, it base64-encodes document.documentElement.outerHTML and POSTs it, along with the logged-in username, to a hardcoded canarytokens.com exfiltration endpoint. It then scrapes the WordPress nonce from /my-account/edit-cuenta/ and issues credentialed POSTs to overwrite the victim's account email to nyxalor_25@proton.me and trigger /wp-login.php?action=lostpassword for account takeover; for a specific target username it instead submits the account-delete form. Every network destination, form field name, and attacker identifier is hidden via \u00XX escapes and "literal".split("").reverse().join("") reversal, with no benign reason to obfuscate fetch targets in a plain npm module. The package advertises no library API and exists solely to deliver this payload via CDN/unpkg-style browser inclusion.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
